skill.name: cold-email
skill.description: Write concise cold emails using AIDA, PAS, and BAB frameworks with personalized openers and clear calls to action.
skill.summary: Create short, personalized cold emails with proven copywriting frameworks.
skill.author: humanizerai

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against SKILL.md and appear to be false positives from copywriting terminology and Markdown formatting. No command execution, cryptographic code, system reconnaissance, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Output Template
low_findings.description: The static command finding points to a Markdown code fence that shows the expected email output format. It does not contain shell commands, command substitution, or instructions to execute code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are a literal email template inside a fenced block. There is no executable command text or user input passed to a shell.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Copywriting Framework Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match prose about the skill description and AIDA copywriting framework. These lines do not contain cryptographic algorithms, hashing functions, or encryption code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear only in normal marketing and copywriting words. The file is an instruction document and includes no cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Personalization Research Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to guidance that the email should show relevant user-provided research. It does not instruct the assistant to inspect the host system or collect local environment data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of cold email personalization advice. No evidence found of host enumeration, filesystem inspection, or environment probing.

content.value_statement: Cold outreach often fails because messages are generic, long, or unclear. This skill turns target, goal, and context details into concise emails with a specific ask.
actual_capabilities: Parses the target, goal, and personalization context from the user prompt.
Chooses between AIDA, PAS, and BAB based on the outreach objective.
Writes subject lines of three to five words in a plain style.
Creates short email bodies designed to stay within 50 to 125 words.
Explains the framework, personalization angle, and word count used.
limitations: It depends on the user to provide accurate target and context details.
It does not verify contact data or company research by itself.
It does not send emails or manage outreach sequences.
It cannot guarantee replies or sales outcomes.

use_cases.title: Prospect Outreach
use_cases.description: Create a short first-touch email for a sales prospect using a relevant pain point and one clear meeting ask.

use_cases.title: Partnership Introduction
use_cases.description: Draft a concise note to a possible partner that explains mutual value without using generic partnership language.

use_cases.title: Feedback Request
use_cases.description: Write a respectful email asking an expert or customer for feedback on a product, message, or idea.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cold Email
prompt_templates.scenario: Start with a simple target and goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a cold email to a VP of Sales at a mid-market SaaS company. Goal: book a 15-minute discovery call. Context: they are hiring more SDRs this quarter.

prompt_templates.title: Personalized Prospect Email
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a cold email to the Head of Marketing at Acme. Goal: introduce our customer research platform. Context: they published a post about reducing churn last week.

prompt_templates.title: Framework-Specific Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the PAS framework to write a cold email for a cybersecurity buyer. Goal: book a demo. Keep the tone direct and calm. Context: they just expanded into financial services.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Revision Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this cold email to fit the skill rules. Keep it under 100 words, add a stronger first line, and replace the vague CTA with one specific ask: [paste draft].

output_examples.input: Target: CFO at a logistics company. Goal: meeting. Context: fuel costs rose in their latest earnings call.
output_examples.output:
Subject: fuel cost visibility

Hi Dana,

I saw your latest earnings call mentioned pressure from fuel costs. We help logistics teams see route-level cost changes earlier, so finance can flag margin risk before month end.

Would it be useful to compare notes for 15 minutes next week?

Alex

Framework used: PAS. Personalization: recent earnings call. Word count: 59.

output_examples.input: Target: Head of People at a growing startup. Goal: intro. Context: they announced hiring 80 people.
output_examples.output:
Subject: scaling onboarding

Hi Priya,

Congrats on the 80-person hiring plan. That kind of growth can make onboarding feel different every week.

We help People teams standardize the first 30 days without adding manual admin work.

Open to a short intro Tuesday or Wednesday?

Morgan

Framework used: BAB. Personalization: hiring announcement. Word count: 63.

best_practices: Provide a specific target, business goal, and one real personalization detail.
Ask for one email at a time when quality matters.
Review the final draft for accuracy, consent rules, and brand voice.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic outreach with no target or context.
Do not use inflated claims, fake personalization, or invented social proof.
Do not send drafts without checking local email compliance requirements.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the recipient role, company or industry, outreach goal, and one specific personalization detail.

faq.question: Can it choose the best framework?
faq.answer: Yes. It selects AIDA, PAS, or BAB based on the goal and context you provide.

faq.question: Does it send the email?
faq.answer: No. It only drafts the email and explains the framework, personalization, and word count.

faq.question: Can it improve an existing draft?
faq.answer: Yes. Paste the draft and ask it to shorten, personalize, or clarify the call to action.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated industries?
faq.answer: It can draft messages, but you should review legal, privacy, and compliance requirements before sending.

faq.question: Will it guarantee replies?
faq.answer: No. It can improve clarity and relevance, but response rates depend on offer, targeting, timing, and trust.
